source.title: Vessel Collision at Energy XXI High Island 154 'B' Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, High Island Block 154

actions_taken: Platform shut in and unmanned at time of collision
BSEE conducted overflight on 23-Jan-2015 to confirm no pollution
event.summary: On 22-Jan-2015 at approximately 1454 hours, the United States flagged Motor Vessel MAERSK KENTUCKY (container ship) collided with Energy XXI's High Island 154 'B' platform. The vessel had dropped anchor in the Fairway Anchorage area approximately 0.9 miles northeast of the platform. The collision occurred on the Starboard side of the container ship in the vicinity of Bay 38. The platform was shut in and unmanned at the time of the collision. The wells, surface equipment, and pipelines were in a condition which prevented a pollution event. The boat landing, caisson riser, various process vessels and associated piping, support beams, handrails, grating, and navaids sustained significant damage estimated at $4,000,000.
recommendations: Improve anchor watch procedures and monitoring protocols for vessels in fairway anchorage areas
Enhanced weather monitoring and response procedures for vessel bridge teams
Faster engine startup procedures or alternative vessel maneuvering capabilities
key_phrases: Officer of Watch failed to plot position readings

threats.name: Officer of Watch Fails to Monitor Position
Vessel Unable to Maneuver Due to Engine Shutdown

controls.name: Well Barriers - SCSSV and SSV
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of containment from well
controls.lod_basis: Subsurface safety valve and surface safety valve provide primary containment barrier

supporting_text: The surface controlled subsurface safety valve (SCSSV) was verified to be disconnected from supply pressure and closed.
The surface safety valve (SSV) was verified to be closed.
The wells, surface equipment, and pipelines were in a condition which prevented a pollution event.

controls.name: Well Temporary Abandonment Barriers
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of containment from temporarily abandoned well
controls.lod_basis: Two barriers of isolation per 30 CFR 250.1721

supporting_text: The B002 well was documented to be temporarily abandoned with two barriers of isolation as per 30 CFR 250.1721.

controls.name: Anchor Watch Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect and prevent vessel dragging anchor
controls.lod_basis: Master's Standing Orders require position plotting at 20-minute intervals

supporting_text: The OOW failed to plot Latitude, Longitude, Radar, and Position readings at twenty minute intervals as directed by the Masters Standing Orders.
The OOW failed to identify the container ship was dragging anchor in time to avoid the collision with HI 154 'B'.
The probable cause of the accident: The OOW failed to maintain a proper Anchor Watch.

controls.name: Weather Monitoring and Response
controls.barrier_role: Monitor weather changes and adjust vessel operations accordingly
controls.lod_basis: USCG Broadcast Notice to Mariners issued for gale force winds

supporting_text: The vessel's bridge team failed to monitor and adjust to changes in weather conditions as required.
The USCG issued a Broadcast Notice to Mariners advising that winds were forecast to shift from East to North and increase to possible gale force Thursday afternoon and into the night.
Contributing cause: The vessel's bridge team failed to monitor and adjust to changes in weather conditions as required.

controls.name: Vessel Engine Availability
controls.barrier_role: Enable vessel maneuvering to avoid collision
controls.lod_basis: Engines required for vessel maneuvering away from platform

supporting_text: Since the vessel's engines had been shut down approximately 30 minutes after the vessel anchored, the crew was unable to maneuver the vessel away from Energy XXI's HI 154 'B' platform prior to the collision.

The crew was unable to gain control of the vessel in time to maneuver away from Energy XXI's HI 154 'B' platform because the engines had been shut down. Engine start up normally takes 20 to 30 minutes.

controls.name: Platform Shutdown and Unmanned Status
controls.barrier_role: Reduce consequences of collision by securing wells and equipment
controls.lod_basis: Platform shut in and unmanned reduces operational risk during collision

supporting_text: The platform was shut in and unmanned at the time of the collision.
The incident investigation determined that at the time of the collision the wells, surface equipment, and pipelines were in a condition which prevented a pollution event.

controls.name: Annual Production Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Verify well barriers and equipment condition
controls.lod_basis: Inspection verifies barrier integrity and equipment status

supporting_text: The last annual production complete inspection by the Lake Jackson District was performed on 29-Oct-2014.
The production inspection documented that the B001 well utilized 2 barriers of protection to secure the well.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
